title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides comprehensive guidance for implementing the **Core Information Standard v2.0**, a foundational health information model developed by the Professional Record Standards Body (PRSB) in the United Kingdom. This standard establishes the essential data elements required for person-centered health and care records across the NHS and broader health and social care ecosystem.

The Core Information Standard serves as a cornerstone for interoperable health information exchange, ensuring that critical patient information is consistently captured, structured, and shared across different care settings. It encompasses demographic data, clinical information, social care requirements, and person-centered elements that support holistic care delivery.

## Background and Context

### Health System Context

The UK health system faces increasing demands from an aging population, rising prevalence of chronic conditions, and the need for integrated care across multiple providers. The fragmentation of health records across primary care, secondary care, social services, and community providers has historically created barriers to effective care coordination. Patients often experience repetitive information gathering, delayed transfers of care, and safety risks due to incomplete information availability at the point of care.

### Policy Drivers

Several key policy initiatives drive the adoption of standardized health information models:

- **NHS Long Term Plan** - Emphasizes digital transformation and integrated care systems
- **Data Saves Lives Strategy** - Promotes standardized data capture and sharing
- **Integrated Care Systems (ICS)** - Requires seamless information flow across organizational boundaries
- **Person-Centered Care Initiatives** - Mandate inclusion of patient preferences and social circumstances

### Clinical and Operational Challenges

Healthcare professionals face daily challenges including:
- Incomplete patient information during emergency admissions
- Lack of visibility into social care arrangements and safeguarding concerns
- Inconsistent medication reconciliation across care transitions
- Limited access to patient preferences and advance care planning documentation
- Fragmented risk assessments and care plans

## Relationship to Clinical Guidelines

The Core Information Standard complements and enables the implementation of numerous clinical guidelines by providing the structured data foundation necessary for evidence-based care. It aligns with:

- **NICE Guidelines** - Supports quality standards through consistent data capture
- **WHO International Standards** - Compatible with international classification systems (ICD-11, ICF)
- **SMART Guidelines** - Provides the data layer for implementing computable guidelines
- **Specialty-Specific Standards** - Extensible to support domain-specific requirements

## Document Overview

This DAK translates the Core Information Standard into actionable implementation guidance, including:

- **Clinical recommendations** mapped to standard data elements
- **User personas and workflows** demonstrating real-world usage
- **Data specifications** with terminology bindings and validation rules
- **Decision support logic** for key clinical scenarios
- **Performance indicators** to measure implementation success
- **Technical requirements** for system developers

The following sections provide detailed guidance for each aspect of implementation, supporting both technical teams and clinical stakeholders in achieving successful deployment of standardized, person-centered health records.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Overview

The Core Information Standard enables a comprehensive range of health interventions by ensuring that essential patient information is available at every point of care. The standard supports evidence-based interventions across clinical, social, and person-centered domains.

## Key Clinical Recommendations

### Information Governance and Consent

Healthcare providers must establish clear protocols for obtaining and documenting consent for information sharing. The standard includes specific data elements for recording consent decisions, including situations where individuals lack capacity or where legitimate representatives are involved.

### Reasonable Adjustments and Accessibility

Under the Equality Act 2010, healthcare organizations have a legal duty to provide reasonable adjustments. The standard mandates systematic capture of:
- Communication requirements (e.g., British Sign Language interpreters, easy-read materials)
- Physical accessibility needs (e.g., wheelchair access, adjustable examination tables)
- Sensory impairments requiring environmental modifications
- Cognitive support requirements

## Intervention Categories

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source/Guideline | Evidence Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Demographic Data Verification** | Verify NHS number, name, date of birth, and current address at every encounter | NHS England Data Quality Standards | A |
| **Allergy and Adverse Reaction Screening** | Document all known allergies with causative agents, reaction types, and severity | NICE CG183 Drug Allergy | A |
| **Medication Reconciliation** | Complete medication reconciliation within 24 hours of admission | NICE NG5 Medicines Optimization | A |
| **Safeguarding Assessment** | Screen for safeguarding concerns at initial assessment and when circumstances change | Care Act 2014 Statutory Guidance | Statutory |
| **Mental Capacity Assessment** | Assess and document capacity for specific decisions when doubt exists | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | Statutory |
| **Advance Care Planning** | Offer advance care planning discussions to all patients with life-limiting conditions | NICE QS13 End of Life Care | B |
| **Social Care Needs Assessment** | Conduct holistic assessment including housing, finances, and support networks | Care Act 2014 | Statutory |
| **Risk Assessment** | Systematically assess and document risks to self, from others, and to others | CQC Fundamental Standards | Regulatory |
| **Immunization Status Review** | Review and update immunization records at preventive care encounters | UK Immunization Schedule | A |
| **Family History Documentation** | Record relevant family history for hereditary conditions | NICE CG164 Familial Breast Cancer | B |

### Person-Centered Interventions

The "About Me" section enables several person-centered interventions:

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source/Guideline | Evidence Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Personal Preferences Documentation** | Record what matters most to the person, including daily routines and preferences | NHS Person-Centered Care Framework | Best Practice |
| **Communication Preferences** | Document preferred communication methods and any communication aids required | Accessible Information Standard | Statutory |
| **Support Network Mapping** | Identify and document important relationships and support preferences | NICE NG86 People's Experience | B |
| **Cultural and Spiritual Needs** | Record cultural, religious, and spiritual preferences affecting care delivery | CQC Fundamental Standards | Regulatory |

### Clinical Decision Support Interventions

The standard enables automated clinical decision support for:

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source/Guideline | Evidence Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Drug Interaction Checking** | Alert prescribers to potential drug interactions based on current medications | MHRA Drug Safety Updates | A |
| **Allergy Alerts** | Provide real-time alerts when prescribing contraindicated medications | NICE CG183 | A |
| **Care Plan Reminders** | Generate reminders for care plan reviews and reassessments | Care Programme Approach | Policy |
| **Vaccination Due Alerts** | Alert clinicians when vaccinations are due based on age and risk factors | Green Book | A |

## Implementation Considerations

### Priority Interventions

Organizations should prioritize implementation of interventions based on:
1. **Patient safety impact** - Allergy documentation, medication reconciliation
2. **Regulatory requirements** - Safeguarding, mental capacity assessments
3. **Population health benefits** - Immunizations, screening programs
4. **Person-centered care** - Preferences, communication needs

### Quality Improvement

The standard supports quality improvement initiatives by enabling:
- Systematic data capture for audit and analysis
- Identification of care gaps and unmet needs
- Monitoring of intervention effectiveness
- Benchmarking across organizations

### Integration with Care Pathways

Interventions should be integrated into existing care pathways:
- Emergency admission pathways - Priority on allergies, medications, advance directives
- Discharge planning - Focus on social circumstances, support networks, follow-up arrangements
- Chronic disease management - Emphasis on care plans, self-management support
- End-of-life care - Advance care planning, preferred place of care/death documentation

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Overview

The Core Information Standard serves diverse user groups across health and social care settings. These personas represent key stakeholder groups who interact with the standard in different capacities, from data entry to clinical decision-making.

## Persona Summary Table

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| P001 | General Practitioner (GP) | Primary care physician responsible for comprehensive patient care, chronic disease management, and care coordination | Yes | Clinical assessment, medication management, referral coordination, preventive care, EHR proficiency |
| P002 | Practice Nurse | Registered nurse in primary care supporting chronic disease clinics, immunizations, and health promotion | Yes | Clinical procedures, health education, care planning, immunization administration, data quality |
| P003 | Hospital Consultant | Senior hospital physician specializing in acute and complex care management | Yes | Specialist clinical knowledge, multidisciplinary team leadership, complex decision-making, discharge planning |
| P004 | Emergency Department Clinician | Doctor or advanced practitioner managing acute presentations in emergency settings | Yes | Rapid assessment, emergency procedures, risk stratification, time-critical decision-making |
| P005 | Community Nurse | Registered nurse providing care in patients' homes and community settings | Yes | Holistic assessment, wound care, medication administration, care coordination, safeguarding |
| P006 | Social Worker | Care professional conducting needs assessments and coordinating social care services | Yes | Psychosocial assessment, safeguarding procedures, care planning, multi-agency working |
| P007 | Care Coordinator | Professional responsible for integrating care across multiple providers and services | Yes | Care pathway management, communication skills, resource coordination, data synthesis |
| P008 | Allied Health Professional | Physiotherapist, occupational therapist, or other therapy professional | Yes | Functional assessment, rehabilitation planning, adaptive equipment, goal setting |
| P009 | Mental Health Practitioner | Psychiatrist, psychologist, or mental health nurse managing mental health conditions | Yes | Mental state assessment, risk assessment, therapeutic interventions, capacity assessment |
| P010 | Care Home Manager | Registered manager overseeing residential or nursing home operations | Yes | Care quality oversight, regulatory compliance, staff management, resident advocacy |
| P011 | Patient/Person | Individual receiving care who may access and contribute to their own record | Yes | Health literacy, self-management, communication of preferences, consent provision |
| P012 | Informal Carer | Family member or friend providing unpaid care and support | Yes | Personal care skills, advocacy, emotional support, liaison with services |
| P013 | Pharmacist | Medication expert in hospital or community settings | Yes | Medication review, drug interactions, patient counseling, medication reconciliation |
| P014 | Paramedic | Emergency care professional providing pre-hospital assessment and treatment | Yes | Emergency assessment, life support, rapid decision-making, handover communication |
| P015 | Administrative Staff | Receptionist or clerk managing appointments and basic data entry | No | Data entry accuracy, patient communication, appointment systems, confidentiality |

## Detailed Persona Profiles

### P001: General Practitioner (GP)

Dr. Sarah Chen is a GP partner with 15 years of experience. She manages a patient list of 1,800 people, seeing 30-40 patients daily. Her responsibilities include diagnosing and treating acute and chronic conditions, coordinating care with specialists, and leading practice quality improvement initiatives.

**Key interactions with Core Information Standard:**
- Reviews and updates problem lists during consultations
- Documents medication changes and monitors for interactions
- Records advance care planning discussions
- Updates social circumstances affecting health
- Creates and reviews care plans for complex patients

### P002: Practice Nurse

James Morrison is a practice nurse specializing in chronic disease management. He runs diabetes, asthma, and hypertension clinics, administers immunizations, and performs health checks.

**Key interactions with Core Information Standard:**
- Records vital signs and clinical observations
- Updates immunization records
- Documents lifestyle factors (smoking, alcohol, exercise)
- Identifies and records need for reasonable adjustments
- Contributes to care plan development

### P003: Hospital Consultant

Professor Amira Patel is a consultant geriatrician managing complex elderly patients. She leads a multidisciplinary team and makes critical decisions about treatment escalation and discharge planning.

**Key interactions with Core Information Standard:**
- Reviews comprehensive patient information for clinical decisions
- Documents capacity assessments and best interest decisions
- Updates advance care planning and DNACPR status
- Coordinates complex discharge plans
- Leads multidisciplinary team meetings

### P006: Social Worker

Michael O'Brien is a hospital social worker conducting assessments for patients requiring social care support. He coordinates with multiple agencies to arrange appropriate care packages.

**Key interactions with Core Information Standard:**
- Completes comprehensive social care assessments
- Documents safeguarding concerns and actions
- Records care package details and funding arrangements
- Updates information about informal carers
- Assesses and documents mental capacity for care decisions

### P011: Patient/Person

Maria Rodriguez is a 72-year-old with diabetes, heart failure, and mild cognitive impairment. She wants to maintain independence and be involved in care decisions.

**Key interactions with Core Information Standard:**
- Contributes to "About Me" section
- Reviews and corrects demographic information
- Provides consent for information sharing
- Expresses preferences for care and treatment
- Identifies important relationships and support needs

## Persona Interaction Patterns

### Cross-Setting Collaboration
- **GP ↔ Hospital Consultant**: Referral information, discharge summaries, medication changes
- **Community Nurse ↔ Social Worker**: Joint assessments, care coordination
- **Care Home Manager ↔ GP**: Resident health updates, medication reviews
- **Patient ↔ All Professionals**: Preference communication, consent, care planning participation

### Information Priority by Persona
- **Emergency Clinicians**: Allergies, current medications, advance directives, key contacts
- **Social Care Staff**: Social circumstances, support networks, safeguarding, capacity
- **Primary Care**: Comprehensive view including preventive care, chronic conditions, social factors
- **Patients/Carers**: Understanding conditions, care plans, appointment schedules, support services

title: User Scenarios
content:
## Overview

These user scenarios demonstrate how the Core Information Standard supports real-world care delivery across different settings and situations. Each scenario illustrates the data elements, workflows, and decision points involved in common care interactions.

## Scenario 1: Emergency Admission with Complex Needs

### Narrative

Mrs. Dorothy Thompson, 78, is brought to the Emergency Department by paramedics after a fall at home. She lives alone, has mild dementia, diabetes, and heart failure. Her daughter Jane, who lives 50 miles away, is her primary carer but wasn't present during the fall. Mrs. Thompson appears confused and cannot reliably report her medications or medical history.

### Data Requirements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Immediate Needs** | NHS number verification, allergy status, current medications, advance directives, resuscitation status |
| **Clinical Data** | Problem list (dementia, diabetes, heart failure), recent observations, fall history, cognitive baseline |
| **Social Context** | Lives alone, daughter as primary carer, home environment risks, support services in place |
| **Communication** | Cognitive impairment affecting communication, daughter's contact details as key informant |
| **Safeguarding** | Vulnerability due to dementia, potential self-neglect, need for capacity assessment |

### Personas Involved
- **P014 - Paramedic**: Provides handover including circumstances of fall and initial observations
- **P004 - ED Clinician**: Conducts assessment, reviews comprehensive record, makes admission decision
- **P012 - Informal Carer (Jane)**: Provides collateral history via phone, confirms medication list
- **P009 - Mental Health Practitioner**: Assesses capacity and delirium risk

### Business Process
- Paramedic handover with scene safety assessment
- Triage nurse accesses Core Information record
- ED clinician reviews allergies, medications, and advance care planning
- Contact with daughter to verify information and understand baseline
- Capacity assessment documented
- Admission decision with safeguarding referral

## Scenario 2: Planned Transition from Hospital to Care Home

### Narrative

Mr. George Williams, 85, is ready for discharge after hip replacement surgery. He can no longer manage at home and has agreed to move to a care home. He has Parkinson's disease, requires assistance with daily activities, and takes multiple medications with specific timing requirements. His son Robert is actively involved in his care.

### Data Requirements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Discharge Planning** | Functional abilities, mobility status, equipment needs, medication regime |
| **Care Requirements** | Assistance with washing/dressing, medication prompts, dietary requirements, fall risk |
| **Medical Management** | Parkinson's medication timing critical, post-operative care instructions, follow-up appointments |
| **Legal/Consent** | Capacity for accommodation decision, son has LPA for property/finances |
| **Preferences** | Daily routines, food preferences, social activities enjoyed |

### Personas Involved
- **P003 - Hospital Consultant**: Oversees medical stability and discharge readiness
- **P008 - Physiotherapist**: Assesses mobility and equipment needs
- **P008 - Occupational Therapist**: Evaluates functional abilities and care needs
- **P006 - Social Worker**: Coordinates care home placement and funding
- **P010 - Care Home Manager**: Reviews requirements to ensure appropriate placement
- **P007 - Care Coordinator**: Ensures seamless transition and information transfer

### Business Process
- Multidisciplinary team meeting to plan discharge
- Comprehensive assessment documentation
- Care home assessment visit
- Creation of detailed care transfer summary
- Medication reconciliation with timing instructions
- Handover meeting with care home staff

## Scenario 3: Chronic Disease Management in Primary Care

### Narrative

Mohan Patel, 52, attends his GP practice for annual diabetes review. He works full-time as a bus driver, struggles with diet control, and has recently started experiencing numbness in his feet. His wife usually accompanies him to appointments to help with translation as English is his second language.

### Data Requirements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Clinical Monitoring** | HbA1c trends, blood pressure, weight, foot examination findings, retinal screening status |
| **Lifestyle Factors** | Shift work affecting meal times, limited exercise due to job, cultural dietary preferences |
| **Risk Assessment** | Cardiovascular risk score, diabetic complications screening, occupational health implications |
| **Communication Needs** | Prefers information in Gujarati, wife assists with translation, needs written materials |
| **Care Planning** | Self-management goals, medication optimization, referral for diabetic foot assessment |

### Personas Involved
- **P002 - Practice Nurse**: Conducts diabetic review and health education
- **P001 - GP**: Reviews medications and makes referral decisions
- **P011 - Patient (Mohan)**: Actively participates in goal setting
- **P012 - Informal Carer (Wife)**: Supports communication and lifestyle changes
- **P013 - Pharmacist**: Provides medication review and adherence support

### Business Process
- Pre-appointment blood tests and data gathering
- Structured diabetes review using template
- Communication needs assessment and adjustment
- Collaborative care planning with SMART goals
- Referrals initiated for podiatry and dietetics
- Follow-up arrangements documented

## Scenario 4: Mental Health Crisis with Safeguarding Concerns

### Narrative

Sarah Jenkins, 35, is brought to A&E by police after a suicide attempt. She has a history of bipolar disorder, recent relationship breakdown, and her two children (ages 8 and 10) were present during the incident. She is known to mental health services but disengaged from treatment three months ago.

### Data Requirements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Immediate Risk** | Suicide risk assessment, means used, protective factors, current mental state |
| **Safeguarding** | Children witnessed attempt, assessment of parenting capacity, child protection considerations |
| **Mental Health History** | Previous admissions, medication history, trigger factors, past risk behaviors |
| **Social Situation** | Recent relationship breakdown, housing stability, support network, employment status |
| **Legal Status** | Capacity for treatment decisions, need for Mental Health Act assessment |

### Personas Involved
- **P004 - ED Clinician**: Initial medical assessment and stabilization
- **P009 - Mental Health Practitioner**: Risk assessment and mental state examination
- **P006 - Social Worker**: Child safeguarding assessment and family support
- **P001 - GP**: Provides background information and medication history
- **Police Officer**: Provides circumstances and scene information

### Business Process
- Immediate medical assessment and treatment
- Psychiatric assessment with risk formulation
- Safeguarding referral for children
- Capacity assessment for treatment decisions
- Crisis plan development with safety planning
- Coordination with children's services
- Follow-up arrangements with crisis team

## Scenario 5: End-of-Life Care Planning

### Narrative

John McCarthy, 72, has advanced lung cancer and wants to plan for his end-of-life care. He wishes to die at home, has specific views about life-sustaining treatments, and wants to ensure his wishes are known across all services. His partner David is supportive but struggling emotionally.

### Data Requirements

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| **Advance Care Planning** | Treatment preferences, DNACPR decision, preferred place of death, advance statement |
| **Clinical Status** | Prognosis, symptom burden, current and anticipated care needs |
| **Support Network** | Partner as main carer, carer assessment needs, family dynamics, bereavement support |
| **Practical Arrangements** | Equipment needs, anticipatory medications, out-of-hours care plan |
| **Coordination** | Hospice involvement, district nursing, GP support, specialist palliative care |

### Personas Involved
- **P003 - Hospital Consultant**: Provides prognosis and treatment options
- **P001 - GP**: Coordinates community care and provides ongoing support
- **P005 - Community Nurse**: Manages symptoms and provides hands-on care
- **Specialist Palliative Care Team**: Supports complex symptom management
- **P011 - Patient (John)**: Actively involved in all planning decisions
- **P012 - Partner (David)**: Requires support as carer

### Business Process
- Advance care planning discussion initiated
- Documented preferences across all settings
- DNACPR form completed and shared
- Electronic palliative care coordination system updated
- Anticipatory medications prescribed and delivered
- Regular review of preferences as condition changes
- 24/7 care plan accessible to all providers

## Cross-Scenario Themes

### Information Criticality
- **Time-critical scenarios**: Allergies, medications, advance directives must be immediately accessible
- **Complex decisions**: Comprehensive social and clinical data supports holistic assessment
- **Care transitions**: Complete, accurate information prevents errors and repetition
- **Person-centered care**: Preferences and "About Me" information maintains dignity and individuality

### Data Quality Impact
- Missing allergy information → potential adverse drug events
- Incomplete social context → inappropriate discharge planning
- Absent advance directives → unwanted interventions
- Poor medication records → therapeutic failures or toxicity

### Workflow Integration
Successful scenarios require:
- Rapid access to core information at point of care
- Clear mechanisms for updating and sharing changes
- Integration with existing clinical systems
- Support for multi-professional collaboration
- Patient and carer involvement in data collection

title: Business Processes & Workflows
content:
## Overview

The Core Information Standard supports critical business processes across health and social care settings. These workflows demonstrate how standardized data collection, sharing, and utilization enable safe, effective, and person-centered care delivery.

## Process 1: Patient Registration and Demographic Verification

**Process ID**: BP001
**Process Title**: New Patient Registration and Identity Verification

### Objectives
Ensure accurate patient identification and demographic data capture to support safe care delivery, reduce duplication, and enable appropriate information sharing across care settings.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP001.01 | Patient presents for registration |
| User Task | BP001.02 | Verify patient identity documents |
| System Task | BP001.03 | Check NHS Spine for existing records |
| User Task | BP001.04 | Capture/update demographic details |
| User Task | BP001.05 | Record contact preferences |
| User Task | BP001.06 | Document communication needs |
| Decision Gateway | BP001.07 | Reasonable adjustments required? |
| User Task | BP001.08 | Record reasonable adjustments |
| System Task | BP001.09 | Generate unique patient record |
| End Event | BP001.10 | Registration complete |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP001.01 | BP001.02 | Always |
| BP001.02 | BP001.03 | Identity verified |
| BP001.03 | BP001.04 | Always |
| BP001.04 | BP001.05 | Demographics captured |
| BP001.05 | BP001.06 | Always |
| BP001.06 | BP001.07 | Always |
| BP001.07 | BP001.08 | Yes - adjustments needed |
| BP001.07 | BP001.09 | No - no adjustments |
| BP001.08 | BP001.09 | Adjustments recorded |
| BP001.09 | BP001.10 | Record created |

## Process 2: Medication Reconciliation at Admission

**Process ID**: BP002
**Process Title**: Medication Reconciliation for Hospital Admission

### Objectives
Accurately identify all medications a patient is taking, resolve discrepancies, and create a complete current medication list to prevent adverse drug events and therapeutic failures.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP002.01 | Patient admitted to hospital |
| User Task | BP002.02 | Review GP medication list |
| User Task | BP002.03 | Interview patient/carer |
| User Task | BP002.04 | Check previous discharge summaries |
| System Task | BP002.05 | Query community pharmacy records |
| User Task | BP002.06 | Compare all sources |
| Decision Gateway | BP002.07 | Discrepancies found? |
| User Task | BP002.08 | Investigate discrepancies |
| User Task | BP002.09 | Document reconciled list |
| User Task | BP002.10 | Prescribe admission medications |
| System Task | BP002.11 | Check for interactions/allergies |
| End Event | BP002.12 | Reconciliation complete |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP002.01 | BP002.02 | Within 24 hours |
| BP002.02 | BP002.03 | Always |
| BP002.03 | BP002.04 | Always |
| BP002.04 | BP002.05 | If available |
| BP002.05 | BP002.06 | Always |
| BP002.06 | BP002.07 | Sources compared |
| BP002.07 | BP002.08 | Yes - discrepancies |
| BP002.07 | BP002.09 | No - all match |
| BP002.08 | BP002.09 | Resolved |
| BP002.09 | BP002.10 | List documented |
| BP002.10 | BP002.11 | Always |
| BP002.11 | BP002.12 | Checks complete |

## Process 3: Safeguarding Assessment and Referral

**Process ID**: BP003
**Process Title**: Adult Safeguarding Screening and Referral

### Objectives
Identify adults at risk of abuse or neglect, document concerns appropriately, and initiate timely referrals to protect vulnerable individuals while respecting autonomy.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP003.01 | Safeguarding concern identified |
| User Task | BP003.02 | Assess immediate safety |
| Decision Gateway | BP003.03 | Immediate danger? |
| User Task | BP003.04 | Take protective action |
| User Task | BP003.05 | Document concern details |
| User Task | BP003.06 | Assess mental capacity |
| Decision Gateway | BP003.07 | Has capacity? |
| User Task | BP003.08 | Discuss with individual |
| User Task | BP003.09 | Follow best interests |
| User Task | BP003.10 | Complete referral form |
| System Task | BP003.11 | Submit to local authority |
| User Task | BP003.12 | Update care plan |
| End Event | BP003.13 | Process complete |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP003.01 | BP003.02 | Immediately |
| BP003.02 | BP003.03 | Assessment done |
| BP003.03 | BP003.04 | Yes - immediate risk |
| BP003.03 | BP003.05 | No - no immediate risk |
| BP003.04 | BP003.05 | Action taken |
| BP003.05 | BP003.06 | Always |
| BP003.06 | BP003.07 | Assessment complete |
| BP003.07 | BP003.08 | Yes - has capacity |
| BP003.07 | BP003.09 | No - lacks capacity |
| BP003.08 | BP003.10 | Discussion complete |
| BP003.09 | BP003.10 | Best interests determined |
| BP003.10 | BP003.11 | Form complete |
| BP003.11 | BP003.12 | Submitted |
| BP003.12 | BP003.13 | Plan updated |

## Process 4: Care Plan Development and Review

**Process ID**: BP004
**Process Title**: Person-Centered Care Plan Creation

### Objectives
Develop comprehensive, person-centered care plans that reflect individual goals, preferences, and needs while coordinating multi-professional inputs and ensuring regular review.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP004.01 | Care planning initiated |
| User Task | BP004.02 | Review assessments |
| User Task | BP004.03 | Explore person's goals |
| User Task | BP004.04 | Identify strengths/assets |
| User Task | BP004.05 | Define SMART objectives |
| User Task | BP004.06 | Assign responsibilities |
| User Task | BP004.07 | Set review date |
| Decision Gateway | BP004.08 | Person agrees? |
| User Task | BP004.09 | Revise plan |
| User Task | BP004.10 | Finalize plan |
| System Task | BP004.11 | Distribute to team |
| Timer Event | BP004.12 | Review date reached |
| User Task | BP004.13 | Conduct review |
| End Event | BP004.14 | Plan active |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP004.01 | BP004.02 | Always |
| BP004.02 | BP004.03 | Assessments reviewed |
| BP004.03 | BP004.04 | Goals explored |
| BP004.04 | BP004.05 | Strengths identified |
| BP004.05 | BP004.06 | Objectives defined |
| BP004.06 | BP004.07 | Responsibilities assigned |
| BP004.07 | BP004.08 | Review date set |
| BP004.08 | BP004.09 | No - changes needed |
| BP004.08 | BP004.10 | Yes - agreed |
| BP004.09 | BP004.03 | Revisions made |
| BP004.10 | BP004.11 | Plan finalized |
| BP004.11 | BP004.14 | Distributed |
| BP004.12 | BP004.13 | Review triggered |
| BP004.13 | BP004.01 | Review complete |

## Process 5: Discharge Planning and Coordination

**Process ID**: BP005
**Process Title**: Hospital Discharge Planning

### Objectives
Ensure safe, timely discharge from hospital with appropriate support services, equipment, and follow-up arrangements in place to prevent readmission.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP005.01 | Discharge planning initiated |
| User Task | BP005.02 | Assess discharge needs |
| User Task | BP005.03 | Evaluate home environment |
| Decision Gateway | BP005.04 | Support needed? |
| User Task | BP005.05 | Arrange services |
| User Task | BP005.06 | Order equipment |
| User Task | BP005.07 | Reconcile medications |
| User Task | BP005.08 | Create discharge summary |
| User Task | BP005.09 | Book follow-up |
| User Task | BP005.10 | Educate patient/carer |
| System Task | BP005.11 | Send summary to GP |
| User Task | BP005.12 | Complete discharge |
| End Event | BP005.13 | Patient discharged |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP005.01 | BP005.02 | 48hrs before discharge |
| BP005.02 | BP005.03 | Needs assessed |
| BP005.03 | BP005.04 | Environment evaluated |
| BP005.04 | BP005.05 | Yes - support required |
| BP005.04 | BP005.07 | No - independent |
| BP005.05 | BP005.06 | Services arranged |
| BP005.06 | BP005.07 | Equipment ordered |
| BP005.07 | BP005.08 | Medications reconciled |
| BP005.08 | BP005.09 | Summary created |
| BP005.09 | BP005.10 | Follow-up booked |
| BP005.10 | BP005.11 | Education complete |
| BP005.11 | BP005.12 | Summary sent |
| BP005.12 | BP005.13 | Discharge complete |

## Process 6: Advance Care Planning Documentation

**Process ID**: BP006
**Process Title**: Advance Care Planning Conversation and Documentation

### Objectives
Facilitate and document advance care planning discussions, ensuring patient preferences for future care are recorded and accessible across all care settings.

### Process Elements

| Type | Element ID | Label |
|---|---|---|
| Start Event | BP006.01 | ACP discussion requested |
| User Task | BP006.02 | Assess readiness |
| User Task | BP006.03 | Explore values/preferences |
| User Task | BP006.04 | Discuss treatment options |
| User Task | BP006.05 | Document preferences |
| Decision Gateway | BP006.06 | DNACPR discussed? |
| User Task | BP006.07 | Complete DNACPR form |
| User Task | BP006.08 | Identify proxy decision maker |
| User Task | BP006.09 | Create advance statement |
| System Task | BP006.10 | Update electronic records |
| User Task | BP006.11 | Share with relevant parties |
| End Event | BP006.12 | ACP documented |

### Process Flow

| From | To | Condition |
|---|---|---|
| BP006.01 | BP006.02 | Always |
| BP006.02 | BP006.03 | Patient ready |
| BP006.03 | BP006.04 | Values explored |
| BP006.04 | BP006.05 | Options discussed |
| BP006.05 | BP006.06 | Preferences documented |
| BP006.06 | BP006.07 | Yes - DNACPR wanted |
| BP006.06 | BP006.08 | No - no DNACPR |
| BP006.07 | BP006.08 | Form completed |
| BP006.08 | BP006.09 | Proxy identified |
| BP006.09 | BP006.10 | Statement created |
| BP006.10 | BP006.11 | Records updated |
| BP006.11 | BP006.12 | Shared appropriately |

## Cross-Process Integration Points

### Data Dependencies
- Registration process creates foundation for all other processes
- Medication reconciliation feeds into discharge planning
- Safeguarding assessments inform care planning
- Advance care planning influences all acute care decisions

### Critical Handoffs
- ED to inpatient teams: medication list, allergies, advance directives
- Hospital to community: discharge summary, care plans, follow-up needs
- Primary to secondary care: problem list, social context, preferences
- Health to social care: functional assessments, safeguarding alerts, capacity decisions

### Quality Checkpoints
- Identity verification before any clinical documentation
- Capacity assessment before major care decisions
- Patient/carer agreement before finalizing plans
- GP notification within 24 hours of discharge

| SOCIAL002 | Housing Type | Type of accommodation | coded | R | House/flat/residential | BP005 | NHS DD accommodation |
| SOCIAL003 | Main Carer | Primary informal carer | string | R | Name + relationship | BP003, BP004 | Relationship codes |
| SOCIAL004 | Carer Assessment | Carer's needs assessed | boolean | R | Y/N + date | BP003 | Care Act 2014 |
| SOCIAL005 | Employment Status | Current work situation | coded | R | Employed/retired etc | BP004 | SNOMED occupation |
| SOCIAL006 | Benefits Received | Welfare benefits | string | R | Benefit types | BP003 | DWP codes |
| SOCIAL007 | Social Isolation Risk | Risk of loneliness | coded | R | Risk level | BP003, BP004 | Local assessment |
| SOCIAL008 | Immigration Status | Legal status in UK | coded | R | Status codes | BP001 | Home Office codes |
| SOCIAL009 | Dependents | Caring responsibilities | string | R | Who + needs | BP003 | Free text |
| SOCIAL010 | Accessing Property | How to access home | text | R | Keys/codes/instructions | BP005 | Free text |

## Personal Preferences (About Me)

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Validation | Linked Process | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PREF001 | Important to Me | What matters most | text | R | Free text | BP004 | Person reported |
| PREF002 | Communication Style | How I communicate | text | R | Free text | BP001, BP004 | Person reported |
| PREF003 | Daily Routine | Typical day structure | text | R | Free text | BP004, BP005 | Person reported |
| PREF004 | Likes and Dislikes | Preferences | text | R | Free text | BP004 | Person reported |
| PREF005 | Cultural Needs | Cultural requirements | text | R | Free text | BP004 | Person reported |
| PREF006 | Spiritual Needs | Faith/spiritual needs | text | R | Free text | BP004, BP006 | Person reported |
| PREF007 | Dietary Preferences | Food preferences/needs | text | R | Free text | BP005 | Person reported |
| PREF008 | Sleep Pattern | Normal sleep routine | text | R | Free text | BP005 | Person reported |
| PREF009 | Mobility Preferences | How I like to move | text | R | Free text | BP005 | Person reported |
| PREF010 | Personal Care | Washing/dressing preferences | text | R | Free text | BP005 | Person reported |

## Data Quality and Governance

### Validation Rules
- **Mandatory fields** must be completed before record can be saved
- **Coded fields** must use specified terminology where available
- **Dates** cannot be future dates unless specifically allowed (e.g., review dates)
- **Clinical measurements** must fall within clinically plausible ranges
- **Cross-field validation** ensures consistency (e.g., end date after start date)

### Terminology Bindings
- **SNOMED CT** - Primary clinical terminology
- **dm+d** - All UK medications and devices
- **LOINC** - Laboratory and observation codes
- **NHS Data Dictionary** - Administrative codes
- **Local codes** - Where national codes unavailable

### Privacy and Security
- **Role-based access** controls who can view/edit elements
- **Audit trail** required for all data changes
- **Consent flags** control information sharing
- **Sensitive data** (e.g., mental health, sexual health) requires additional controls

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Overview

The Core Information Standard enables automated decision support to enhance patient safety, improve care quality, and support clinical decision-making. These decision tables define the logic for key clinical scenarios, triggered by specific data combinations.

## Decision Table 1: Medication Safety Alerts

**Title**: Medication Allergy and Interaction Checking
**Description**: Automatically checks prescribed medications against documented allergies and current medications to prevent adverse drug events.

### Input Elements
- Current medication list (CLIN008)
- Documented allergies (CLIN001, CLIN002, CLIN003)
- New prescription request
- Patient age (calculated from DEM005)
- Renal function (if available)

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| MED001 | New prescription = Penicillin AND Allergy = Penicillin | BLOCK prescription + Alert prescriber | Absolute contraindication |
| MED002 | New prescription = NSAID AND Current meds include ACE inhibitor AND Age > 65 | WARNING - High risk combination | Risk of renal impairment |
| MED003 | New prescription = Warfarin AND Current meds include Antibiotic | WARNING - Check INR | Increased bleeding risk |
| MED004 | New prescription = Metformin AND eGFR < 30 | BLOCK prescription + Suggest alternative | Contraindicated in severe renal impairment |
| MED005 | Allergy severity = "Anaphylaxis" AND Drug class matches | BLOCK + Emergency alert | Life-threatening allergy |
| MED006 | Polypharmacy (>10 meds) AND Age > 75 | PROMPT medication review | High risk of adverse events |
| MED007 | Duplicate therapy detected | WARNING - Verify intentional | Prevent therapeutic duplication |

## Decision Table 2: Safeguarding Risk Assessment

**Title**: Adult Safeguarding Alert Generation
**Description**: Identifies combinations of factors that indicate potential safeguarding concerns requiring immediate action.

### Input Elements
- Living situation (SOCIAL001)
- Mental capacity status (ASSESS012)
- Recent injuries/presentations
- Carer stress indicators
- Previous safeguarding history (LEGAL010)
- Behavioral indicators

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| SAFE001 | Unexplained injury AND Lives with others AND Previous safeguarding alert | IMMEDIATE safeguarding referral | High risk pattern |
| SAFE002 | Lacks capacity AND No LPA AND Financial concerns raised | URGENT capacity assessment + Safeguarding | Financial abuse risk |
| SAFE003 | Multiple ED visits (>3 in month) AND Unexplained symptoms | FLAG for MDT review | Possible neglect/abuse |
| SAFE004 | Carer expressing inability to cope AND Person has high needs | URGENT carer assessment + Support | Prevention opportunity |
| SAFE005 | Self-neglect indicators AND Lacks capacity | Best interests meeting needed | Complex safeguarding |
| SAFE006 | Pressure ulcers AND Care home resident | INVESTIGATE care quality | Possible neglect |
| SAFE007 | Sudden behavior change AND Cognitive impairment | ASSESS for abuse/illness | Multiple causes possible |

## Decision Table 3: Discharge Safety Assessment

**Title**: Safe Discharge Decision Support
**Description**: Evaluates multiple factors to determine if patient is safe for discharge and what support is needed.

### Input Elements
- Mobility assessment (ASSESS013)
- Living situation (SOCIAL001, SOCIAL002)
- Cognitive status (ASSESS012)
- Falls history (ASSESS015)
- Medication changes
- Support network (SOCIAL003)

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| DISCH001 | Lives alone AND Mobility impaired AND No support | BLOCK discharge - Arrange support | Unsafe without help |
| DISCH002 | Falls risk = High AND Stairs at home | OT assessment required | Environmental hazard |
| DISCH003 | >5 medication changes AND Cognitive impairment | ARRANGE pharmacy support | Medication safety |
| DISCH004 | New oxygen therapy AND Lives alone | ENSURE equipment training | Safety critical |
| DISCH005 | Pressure ulcer AND No care package | ARRANGE district nursing | Wound care needed |
| DISCH006 | Palliative AND No anticipatory meds | PRESCRIBE anticipatory care | Prevent crisis |
| DISCH007 | Complex needs AND Carer stress | RESPITE care consideration | Sustainability check |

## Decision Table 4: Capacity and Consent Validation

**Title**: Mental Capacity and Consent Decision Logic
**Description**: Ensures appropriate consent processes based on capacity assessments and identifies when best interests decisions are required.

### Input Elements
- Mental capacity assessment (ASSESS012, LEGAL002)
- Type of decision required
- Advance directives (LEGAL007)
- LPA status (LEGAL004)
- Urgency of decision

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| CAP001 | Major treatment AND No capacity assessment | STOP - Assess capacity first | Legal requirement |
| CAP002 | Lacks capacity AND No LPA AND Non-urgent | ARRANGE best interests meeting | MCA compliance |
| CAP003 | Lacks capacity AND Valid advance directive | FOLLOW advance directive | Legally binding |
| CAP004 | Emergency treatment AND Unknown capacity | PROCEED in best interests + Document | Emergency exception |
| CAP005 | Fluctuating capacity AND Major decision | WAIT for lucid period if possible | Respect autonomy |
| CAP006 | Research participation AND Lacks capacity | CHECK for research-specific consent | Additional safeguards |
| CAP007 | Refusing treatment AND Has capacity | RESPECT decision + Document | Autonomy principle |

## Decision Table 5: Clinical Deterioration Detection

**Title**: Early Warning Score and Escalation
**Description**: Calculates risk scores based on vital signs and triggers appropriate clinical escalation.

### Input Elements
- Vital signs (ASSESS001-006)
- Level of consciousness
- Age (from DEM005)
- Baseline observations
- Oxygen therapy status

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| EWS001 | NEWS2 score ≥ 7 | IMMEDIATE medical review | Critical illness |
| EWS002 | NEWS2 score 5-6 OR single parameter = 3 | URGENT review within 1 hour | Moderate concern |
| EWS003 | Oxygen sats < 92% AND Not on oxygen | START oxygen + Medical review | Hypoxia |
| EWS004 | Systolic BP < 90 OR > 220 | IMMEDIATE medical attention | Cardiovascular instability |
| EWS005 | RR < 8 OR > 25 | URGENT assessment | Respiratory failure risk |
| EWS006 | Temp > 39°C AND Immunosuppressed | SEPSIS protocol activation | High risk group |
| EWS007 | Deteriorating trend over 3 observations | INCREASE monitoring frequency | Early intervention |

## Decision Table 6: Preventive Care Alerts

**Title**: Opportunistic Health Screening Reminders
**Description**: Identifies opportunities for preventive care interventions based on patient characteristics and history.

### Input Elements
- Age and sex (DEM005, DEM007)
- Existing conditions (CLIN005)
- Last screening dates
- Risk factors (smoking, BMI, family history)
- Immunization history (CLIN013)

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| PREV001 | Age ≥ 50 AND No bowel screening recorded | PROMPT bowel cancer screening | Population screening |
| PREV002 | Female AND Age 50-70 AND >3 years since mammogram | PROMPT breast screening | NHS programme |
| PREV003 | Diabetes AND No retinal screen in 12 months | URGENT referral to eye screening | Prevent complications |
| PREV004 | Age ≥ 65 AND No flu vaccine this season | OFFER flu vaccination | High risk group |
| PREV005 | Smoker AND No cessation support offered | OFFER smoking cessation | Every contact counts |
| PREV006 | BMI > 30 AND No weight management discussed | PROMPT healthy weight discussion | Prevention opportunity |
| PREV007 | Age ≥ 75 AND No frailty assessment | CONDUCT frailty scoring | Proactive care |

## Decision Table 7: End-of-Life Care Triggers

**Title**: Palliative and End-of-Life Care Recognition
**Description**: Identifies patients who may benefit from palliative care approach or end-of-life planning.

### Input Elements
- Diagnosis (CLIN005)
- Hospital admissions frequency
- Functional decline (ASSESS013)
- Surprise question response
- Weight loss pattern
- Advance care planning status (LEGAL005, CARE005)

### Decision Logic

| Condition ID | Inputs | Output Action | Annotation |
|---|---|---|---|
| EOL001 | "Would not be surprised if died within year" = Yes | INITIATE advance care planning | Clinical trigger |
| EOL002 | ≥3 admissions in 6 months AND Progressive disease | REFER palliative care team | Complex needs |
| EOL003 | Metastatic cancer AND No ACP documented | URGENT ACP discussion | Time sensitive |
| EOL004 | COPD + Home oxygen AND No ceiling of care | DISCUSS treatment limits | Prevent inappropriate care |
| EOL005 | Dementia stage 7 AND Recurrent infections | CONSIDER comfort care focus | Natural progression |
| EOL006 | Weight loss >10% AND No reversible cause | ASSESS for EOL care needs | Prognostic indicator |
| EOL007 | Preferred place of death recorded AND Not achievable | REVIEW and update plan | Manage expectations |

## Implementation Considerations

### Alert Fatigue Management
- Suppress duplicate alerts within 24-hour period
- Allow clinician override with reason documentation
- Regular review of alert effectiveness
- Customizable alert thresholds by organization

### Clinical Override
- All automated decisions can be overridden by clinicians
- Override reasons must be documented
- Regular audit of overrides to refine logic

### Integration Requirements
- Real-time access to current data elements
- Sub-second response time for safety alerts
- Clear visual distinction between alert severity levels
- Mobile-friendly alert display

### Continuous Improvement
- Monthly review of alert patterns
- Clinician feedback incorporation
- Regular updates based on new evidence
- Machine learning for pattern refinement

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Overview

These indicators measure the implementation success, data quality, and clinical outcomes associated with the Core Information Standard. They support continuous improvement, regulatory compliance, and demonstration of value to stakeholders.

## Core Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CQI001 | NHS Number Completeness | Patients with valid NHS number recorded | All registered patients | By practice/ward, age group | Monthly | Patient records |
| CQI002 | Allergy Documentation Rate | Patients with allergy status documented (including "no known allergies") | All patients with clinical contact in period | By setting, clinician type | Weekly | Clinical records |
| CQI003 | Medication Reconciliation Timeliness | Admissions with medications reconciled within 24 hours | All hospital admissions | By ward, day of week | Daily | Admission data |
| CQI004 | Care Plan Currency | Patients with care plan reviewed within specified timeframe | All patients with active care plans | By condition, team | Monthly | Care planning system |
| CQI005 | Consent Documentation Completeness | Decisions with appropriate consent recorded | All significant care decisions | By decision type, capacity status | Monthly | Consent records |

| OUT002 | Medication Harm Reduction | Adverse drug events reported | All patients on medications | By severity, preventability | Monthly | Incident reports |
| OUT003 | Care Plan Goal Achievement | Care plan goals achieved or partially achieved | All care plan goals set | By goal type, timeframe | Quarterly | Care plan reviews |
| OUT004 | Emergency Attendance Reduction | Reduction in ED attendances for cohort | Baseline ED attendance rate | By patient group, intervention | Quarterly | ED data |
| OUT005 | Patient Satisfaction | Patients rating care coordination as good/excellent | All patients surveyed | By aspect of care | Quarterly | Patient survey |
| OUT006 | Carer Wellbeing | Carers reporting reduced burden | All carers surveyed | By support received | Annual | Carer survey |

## Data Quality Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DQ001 | Core Data Completeness | Mandatory fields with valid values | All mandatory fields | By section, data entry point | Daily | Data quality dashboard |
| DQ002 | Terminology Adherence | Coded entries using standard terminologies | All coded data fields | By terminology, user group | Weekly | Terminology services |
| DQ003 | Data Freshness | Data elements updated within required timeframe | All time-sensitive elements | By element type, responsible team | Daily | Audit timestamps |
| DQ004 | Cross-System Consistency | Data elements matching across systems | Shared data elements | By system pair, element | Weekly | Integration monitoring |
| DQ005 | Validation Rule Compliance | Data entries passing validation rules | All data entries | By rule type, entry method | Daily | Validation logs |
| DQ006 | Audit Trail Completeness | Changes with complete audit information | All data changes | By change type, user role | Monthly | Audit system |

## Implementation Progress Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| IMP001 | User Training Completion | Staff completing required training | All staff requiring access | By role, department | Weekly | Training records |
| IMP002 | System Integration Status | Data elements successfully mapped | All required data elements | By system, interface | Monthly | Integration testing |
| IMP003 | Workflow Adoption Rate | Workflows using standardized processes | All relevant workflows | By process, department | Monthly | Process audit |
| IMP004 | Stakeholder Engagement | Stakeholders actively using system | All identified stakeholders | By stakeholder type | Monthly | Usage statistics |
| IMP005 | Issue Resolution Time | Issues resolved within SLA | All reported issues | By severity, type | Weekly | Issue tracking |
| IMP006 | Benefits Realization | Expected benefits achieved | All planned benefits | By benefit category | Quarterly | Benefits tracking |

## Dashboard and Reporting

### Real-Time Monitoring
- **Safety alerts dashboard** - Live view of triggered alerts and responses
- **Data quality monitor** - Real-time completeness and accuracy metrics
- **User activity tracker** - Current system usage and performance

### Regular Reports
- **Weekly operational report** - Key metrics for clinical teams
- **Monthly quality report** - Comprehensive quality and safety analysis
- **Quarterly strategic report** - Progress against strategic objectives
- **Annual outcomes report** - Population health impact assessment

### Benchmarking
- Compare performance across teams, departments, and organizations
- National and regional benchmark participation
- Continuous improvement targets based on top quartile performance
- Sharing best practices from high performers

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Overview

This section defines the functional and non-functional requirements for implementing the Core Information Standard. These requirements ensure systems can effectively capture, store, share, and utilize standardized patient information while meeting performance, security, and usability needs.

## Functional Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| FR001 | Patient Identity Management | System must support NHS number validation, demographic data capture, and identity verification including photo capture and biometric options | BP001 | Critical |
| FR002 | Multi-Source Data Integration | Ability to integrate data from GP systems, hospital EPRs, community systems, and social care platforms in real-time | All processes | Critical |
| FR003 | Comprehensive Assessment Tools | Provide structured forms for clinical, functional, and social assessments with mandatory field validation and skip logic | BP004, BP005 | High |
| FR004 | Medication Management | Support medication reconciliation workflows, allergy checking, interaction alerts, and structured dose syntax | BP002 | Critical |
| FR005 | Consent Management | Capture and track consent decisions for information sharing, treatment, and research with audit trail | BP001, BP003, BP006 | Critical |
| FR006 | Care Plan Builder | Enable collaborative care planning with SMART goals, assigned actions, progress tracking, and review scheduling | BP004 | High |
| FR007 | Clinical Decision Support | Real-time alerts for allergies, drug interactions, safeguarding risks, and clinical deterioration based on decision tables | All processes | Critical |
| FR008 | Document Management | Store and retrieve clinical documents, images, and multimedia files with appropriate metadata and access controls | All processes | High |
| FR009 | Referral Management | Create, send, track, and receive referrals with all required information and acknowledgment workflows | BP005 | High |
| FR010 | Safeguarding Workflows | Support safeguarding assessments, referrals, multi-agency communication, and outcome tracking | BP003 | Critical |
| FR011 | Person-Centered Data Capture | Enable patients/carers to contribute "About Me" information, preferences, and goals through assisted or self-service interfaces | BP004 | High |
| FR012 | Advance Care Planning | Document and share advance directives, DNACPR decisions, preferred place of care/death with version control | BP006 | Critical |
| FR013 | Discharge Planning Tools | Coordinate discharge requirements including equipment, services, medications, and follow-up with checklist validation | BP005 | High |
| FR014 | Multi-Disciplinary Team Support | Enable secure communication, shared records access, and meeting documentation for care teams | BP004, BP005 | High |
| FR015 | Appointment Scheduling | Book, reschedule, and track appointments across services with integration to patient preferences | All processes | Medium |
| FR016 | Risk Assessment Tools | Standardized assessments for falls, pressure ulcers, malnutrition, frailty with automatic scoring | BP003, BP004 | High |
| FR017 | Reporting and Analytics | Generate operational reports, quality indicators, and population health analytics from standardized data | All processes | High |
| FR018 | Mobile Access | Full functionality on mobile devices for community staff with offline capability and synchronization | BP005 | High |
| FR019 | Integration APIs | FHIR R4 compliant APIs for data exchange with third-party systems and national infrastructure | All processes | Critical |
| FR020 | Audit and Compliance | Comprehensive audit logging, consent tracking, and regulatory reporting capabilities | All processes | Critical |

## Non-Functional Requirements

| Requirement ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| NFR001 | Response Time | Page load time <2 seconds, search results <3 seconds, alert generation <1 second for critical alerts | Performance | Critical |
| NFR002 | System Availability | 99.9% uptime during core hours (8am-8pm), 99.5% overall, with planned maintenance windows | Availability | Critical |
| NFR003 | Concurrent Users | Support minimum 10,000 concurrent users across the integrated care system without degradation | Scalability | Critical |
| NFR004 | Data Volume | Handle 5 million patient records with average 100 data points per record and 10GB documents per record | Scalability | High |
| NFR005 | Security Standards | NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit compliance, Cyber Essentials Plus certification, ISO 27001 | Security | Critical |
| NFR006 | Authentication | Integration with NHS Identity (NHS CIS2/Okta), smartcard support, biometric options, SSO across systems | Security | Critical |
| NFR007 | Data Encryption | AES-256 encryption at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, encrypted backups, key management service | Security | Critical |
| NFR008 | Access Control | Role-based access control (RBAC) with legitimate relationship verification, break-glass procedures | Security | Critical |
| NFR009 | Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, screen reader support, keyboard navigation, adjustable fonts/contrast | Usability | Critical |
| NFR010 | Multi-Language Support | Interface in English, Welsh, and top 10 community languages, with clinical content translation | Usability | High |
| NFR011 | Training Time | New users operational within 4 hours training, role-specific modules, in-app guidance | Usability | High |
| NFR012 | Disaster Recovery | RPO (Recovery Point Objective) of 1 hour, RTO (Recovery Time Objective) of 4 hours | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR013 | Interoperability | HL7 FHIR R4 UK Core, NHS Data Dictionary compliance, SNOMED CT/dm+d native support | Interoperability | Critical |
| NFR014 | Clinical Safety | DCB0129/DCB0160 compliance, clinical risk management, hazard log maintenance | Safety | Critical |
| NFR015 | Performance Degradation | Graceful degradation under load, automatic scaling, queue management for batch processes | Performance | High |
| NFR016 | Mobile Performance | Offline capability for 48 hours, selective sync, conflict resolution, battery optimization | Mobility | High |
| NFR017 | Backup and Archive | Daily incremental backups, weekly full backups, 7-year archive retention, tested restoration | Reliability | Critical |
| NFR018 | Monitoring and Alerting | Real-time system monitoring, automated alerting, performance dashboards, SLA tracking | Maintainability | High |
| NFR019 | Patch Management | Security patches within 30 days (7 days for critical), feature updates quarterly, zero-downtime deployment | Maintainability | High |
| NFR020 | Data Quality Validation | Real-time validation rules, duplicate detection, terminology validation, completeness checking | Quality | High |
| NFR021 | Session Management | 20-minute timeout with warning, session persistence, concurrent session controls | Security | High |
| NFR022 | Print Requirements | Formatted printing for care plans, assessments, summaries with patient stickers/labels | Usability | Medium |
| NFR023 | Browser Support | Chrome, Edge, Safari (latest 2 versions), progressive enhancement for older browsers | Compatibility | High |
| NFR024 | Integration Patterns | REST APIs, event streaming, batch interfaces, real-time notifications via webhooks | Integration | High |
| NFR025 | Capacity Planning | Auto-scaling for 200% peak load, performance testing for 5-year growth projection | Scalability | High |

## Technical Architecture Requirements

### Platform Requirements
- Cloud-native architecture (NHS-approved cloud providers)
- Microservices design with containerization
- API-first development approach
- DevOps/CI/CD pipeline automation

### Data Architecture
- Master Data Management (MDM) for patient identity
- Event-driven architecture for real-time updates
- Data lake for analytics and reporting
- Federated query capability across systems

### Integration Requirements
- NHS Spine integration (PDS, SCR, e-RS)
- GP system integration (EMIS, SystmOne, Vision)
- Hospital PAS/EPR integration
- Social care system connectivity
- NHS App integration

### Security Architecture
- Zero-trust network architecture
- API gateway with rate limiting
- Web Application Firewall (WAF)
- Security Information and Event Management (SIEM)
- Regular penetration testing

## Compliance and Standards

### Regulatory Compliance
- UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018
- NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit
- Care Quality Commission standards
- Medical Device Regulations (where applicable)

### Clinical Standards
- PRSB standard compliance
- NICE guidance alignment
- Royal College guidelines
- Professional body requirements

### Technical Standards
- NHS Digital Architecture Principles
- Government Digital Service standards
- Open standards adoption
- OWASP security guidelines

## Implementation Priorities

### Phase 1 - Core Foundation (Months 1-6)
- Patient identity and demographics
- Consent management
- Core clinical data capture
- Basic integration APIs

### Phase 2 - Clinical Safety (Months 4-9)
- Allergy and medication management
- Clinical decision support
- Safeguarding workflows
- Audit and compliance

### Phase 3 - Care Coordination (Months 7-12)
- Care planning tools
- Multi-disciplinary team support
- Discharge planning
- Person-centered features

### Phase 4 - Advanced Features (Months 10-18)
- Advanced analytics
- Mobile applications
- Patient portal
- Population health management
